project: AI Video Production Workspace Three Route Plan
title: RunningHub Regeneration Authorization Prep
scope: prepare one coherent local authorization package for regenerating all 4 revision_needed shots through the RunningHub primary lane
merge R3-9G regeneration strategies for g0_r1_shot_001, g0_r1_shot_003, and g0_r1_shot_004 with the R3-9H same-keyframe repair recommendation for g0_r1_shot_002
produce a dry-run execution plan, budget boundary, output directory plan, and exact future authorization phrase draft

do not read env files or credentials, call providers, upload media, submit jobs, poll status, download provider outputs, regenerate clips, batch-expand, assemble final video, mutate the frozen storyboard package, or overwrite source assets
goal: Prepare a local-only, auditable RunningHub regeneration authorization package for the four rejected or regeneration-requested clips before any paid live execution.
acceptance: R3-9F, R3-9G, and R3-9H reports are parsed as the source of truth.
Plan includes exactly 4 shots: g0_r1_shot_001, g0_r1_shot_002, g0_r1_shot_003, and g0_r1_shot_004.
SHOT_001 plan preserves the lunchbox on the table and requires food to be picked from inside the lunchbox and brought to the mouth.
SHOT_002 plan uses the same storyboard image artifact and explicitly forbids sighing, unhappy expression, slumped posture, disappointment, fatigue, or product-negative mood.
SHOT_003 plan requires realistic cap fold behavior where folds become shallower and fabric responds to pull direction.
SHOT_004 plan requires physically consistent cap lighting, shadow direction, fabric texture, and contact shadow.

Each shot records source storyboard image artifact id, rejected generated clip artifact id, revised prompt guidance, revised negative constraints, duration_seconds=6, aspectRatio=9:16, resolution=480p, and isolated output_dir.

Budget boundary records max_upload_calls_total=4, max_submit_calls_total=4, max one upload and one submit per shot, no retry, no second submit, no Runway fallback, no batch expansion, and stop-on-first upload or submit failure.
Report includes a future exact authorization phrase draft but does not execute it.
Report states final assembly remains blocked until regenerated clips are reviewed and accepted by a later human review task.

Report records network_call_attempted=false, runninghub_called=false, runway_called=false, credentials_read=false, env_files_read=false, provider_credits_consumed=false, real_video_generated=false, regeneration_performed=false, final_assembly_performed=false, source_assets_overwritten=false, secret_values_exposed=false.
validation: JSON parse for generated R3-9I authorization prep report
boundary_or_safety_stop: prep only; no provider call, env or credential read, regeneration execution, final assembly, source overwrite, or deployment
safe_actions_completed: Merged R3-9G strategies for g0_r1_shot_001, g0_r1_shot_003, and g0_r1_shot_004 with R3-9H same-keyframe repair for g0_r1_shot_002.
Prepared a bounded 4-shot RunningHub regeneration authorization phrase draft with max 4 uploads and max 4 submits.
Validated JSON parse, npm run r3:9i:prep, npm run typecheck, npm run test:m1, npm run secret:scan, and git diff --check.
options_for_jenn: After R3-9I completes, review the exact authorization phrase draft before any real RunningHub regeneration.
A future live regeneration task must require fresh exact current authorization and must cap execution to 4 uploads and 4 submits total.
next_safe_option: Do not execute live regeneration until Jenn provides a new exact authorization phrase.
